title: Smoky Roasted Silky Indian Chicken Curry
description: A monsoon-inspired, halal-friendly chicken curry dish from Kerala, India, featuring a smoky roasted flavor and a silky texture.

ingredients.name: chicken drumsticks
ingredients.notes: halal and bone-in for better flavor

ingredients.name: tomato puree
ingredients.notes: diced or crushed tomatoes also work

ingredients.name: shio-koji cure
ingredients.notes: or 1 tsp salt and 1 tsp sugar if you don't have shio-koji

instructions: Rinse the chicken drumsticks under cold water and pat them dry with paper towels.
In a small bowl, mix the shio-koji cure, coriander powder, cumin powder, turmeric powder, and red chili powder.
Rub the spice mixture evenly onto all sides of the chicken drumsticks.
Heat 1 tablespoon of coconut oil in a large oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat. Add the chicken drumsticks and sear until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes per side.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and roast for 20 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through.
Meanwhile, heat the remaining 1 tablespoon of coconut oil in a small saucepan over medium heat. Add the onion, ginger, and garlic and sauté until the onion is translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the tomato puree, ketchup, and curry leaves (if using) to the saucepan and stir to combine.
Bring the sauce to a simmer and cook for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce has thickened and reduced slightly.
Once the chicken is done roasting, remove the skillet from the oven and carefully place it on top of the stovetop over low heat.
Pour the curry sauce into the skillet and stir to combine with the chicken and juices.
Simmer the curry for 5-10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce has thickened and the chicken is fully coated.
Garnish with curry leaves and serve hot.
